session.user:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
I need to understand the following for implementing remote dispatch in `pr dispatch`:
1. What does `IssueInfo` look like in `src/erk/gateway/remote_github.py`? Does it have `head_ref_name`? 2. What does `RemoteGitHub` look like - especially `get_issue()`, `create_file_commit()`, `dispatch_workflow()`, `update_pull_request_body()`, `add_issue_comment()`, `get_default_branch_name()` methods? 3. What does `ValidatedPlannedPR` look like? 4. What does `build_impl_context_files()` look like? 5. Is there an existing `extract_plan_from_body()` function or equivalent? 6. What does `repo_option` and `resolve_owner_repo` look like in repo_resolution.py? 7. How does `one_shot_remote_dispatch.py` work (the existing remote pattern)? 8. What does `NoRepoSentinel` look like?
Provide full file paths and key code snippets for each.
session.assistant: I'll help you understand the implementation details for remote dispatch in `pr dispatch`. Let me start by checking the documentation and then exploring the codebase systematically.

1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)

1→--- 2→title: One-Shot Workflow 3→last_audited: "2026-02-24 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "working with erk one-shot dispatch" 7→ - "understanding how plans are autonomously created and implemented" 8→ - "debugging one-shot workflow failures" 9→ - "integrating objectives with one-shot dispatch" 10→tripwires: 11→ - action: "adding dry-run support to one-shot commands" 12→ warning: "One-shot dry-run mode must NOT create skeleton issues" 13→ - action: "modifying register-one-shot-plan exit behavior" 14→ warning: "register-one-shot-plan uses best-effort: exit 0 if any operation succeeds" 15→ - action: "adding post-dispatch operations without matching dispatch_cmd.py pattern" 16→ warning: "dispatch_one_shot() and _dispatch_planned_pr_plan() in dispatch_cmd.py must stay synchronized. Both use write_dispatch_metadata() + create_submission_queued_block(). Changes to one must be mirrored in the other." 17→ - action: "writing post-dispatch operations without try/except guards" 18→ warning: "Post-dispatch operations (metadata write, queued comment) are best-effort. Wrap in try/except with user-visible warnings. See write_dispatch_metadata() and create_submission_queued_block() in one_shot_dispatch.py." 19→ - action: "editing plan body content in plan creation, replan, or one-shot dispatch" 20→ warning: "One-shot metadata block preservation: the metadata block in the plan body (HTML comment with erk:metadata-block markers) must survive all edits. Never strip or overwrite HTML comment blocks that contain erk:metadata-block markers." 21→ score: 9 22→--- 23→ 24→# One-Shot Workflow 25→ 26→The one-shot workflow enables fully autonomous planning and implementation from a single CLI command. It dispatches a GitHub Actions workflow that creates a plan and implements it without human intervention. 27→ 28→## End-to-End Pipeline 29→ 30→```
... [163 lines omitted] ...
104→`src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py` performs four independent operations that `erk pr submit` normally handles at submit time. Each operation is best-effort -- failures are logged but don't block others: 111→The command outputs JSON results for each operation with success/error details. 124→The CLI write is the primary source; CI registration fills gaps when the CLI couldn't complete (e.g., network failure during dispatch).

1→--- 2→title: dispatch_ref Configuration 3→read_when: 4→ - "configuring which branch workflow_dispatch targets" 5→ - "working with .erk/config.toml dispatch settings" 6→ - "debugging workflow dispatch targeting the wrong branch" 7→ - "using --ref CLI option to override dispatch branch per run" 8→ - "using --ref-current to dispatch against current branch" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "assuming dispatch_ref is project-level config" 11→ warning: "dispatch_ref is repo-level config (.erk/config.toml), overridable at local level (.erk/config.local.toml). It is not project-level." 12→ - action: "passing both --ref and --ref-current to a dispatch command" 13→ warning: "--ref and --ref-current are mutually exclusive. resolve_dispatch_ref() raises UsageError if both are provided." 14→--- 15→ 16→# dispatch_ref Configuration 17→ 18→Override the default branch used for GitHub Actions `workflow_dispatch` events. By default, erk dispatches workflows against the repository's default branch (usually `master`). The `dispatch_ref` config allows targeting a different branch. 19→ 20→## Configuration 21→ 22→Top-level key in `.erk/config.toml`: 23→ 24→```toml 25→dispatch_ref = "my-custom-branch" 26→``` 27→ 28→Can be overridden per-user in `.erk/config.local.toml`: 29→ 30→```toml
... [89 lines omitted] ...
75→**Mutual exclusivity:** `--ref` and `--ref-current` cannot be used together. `resolve_dispatch_ref()` raises `UsageError` if both are provided. 77→**Detached HEAD:** Using `--ref-current` in a detached HEAD state raises a `UsageError` with a descriptive message, since there is no current branch to resolve.
No files found

1→"""Abstract interface for remote GitHub operations without a local git clone. 2→ 3→This gateway enables GitHub operations (branch creation, file commits, PR management, 4→workflow dispatch) using the GitHub REST API directly, without requiring a local 5→git repository. All methods take explicit owner/repo parameters. 6→""" 7→ 8→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 9→ 10→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo, IssueNotFound, PRReference 11→ 12→ 13→class RemoteGitHub(ABC): 14→ """GitHub operations that don't require a local git clone. 15→ 16→ Every method takes explicit owner/repo parameters instead of repo_root: Path. 17→ Uses the GitHub REST API via HttpClient. 18→ """ 19→ 20→ @abstractmethod 21→ def get_authenticated_user(self) -> str: 22→ """Get the username of the authenticated GitHub user. 23→ 24→ Returns: 25→ GitHub username string 26→ """ 27→ ... 28→ 29→ @abstractmethod 30→ def get_default_branch_name(self, *, owner: str, repo: str) -> str:

1→"""Draft PR implementation of plan storage. 2→ 3→Uses GitHub draft pull requests as the backing store for plans. 4→PR body contains the plan-header metadata block followed by plan content. 5→The "erk-plan" label identifies plan PRs vs regular draft PRs. 6→""" 7→ 8→import logging 9→from collections.abc import Mapping 10→from datetime import UTC 11→from pathlib import Path 12→ 13→from erk_shared.gateway.github.abc import LocalGitHub 14→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues 15→from erk_shared.gateway.github.label_ops import add_labels_resilient 16→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( 17→ add_metadata_block, 18→ find_metadata_block, 19→ has_metadata_block, 20→ render_metadata_block, 21→ replace_metadata_block_in_body, 22→) 23→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.plan_header import ( 24→ format_plan_header_body, 25→) 26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import ( 27→ CREATED_FROM_SESSION, 28→ CREATED_FROM_WORKFLOW_RUN_URL, 29→ LEARNED_FROM_ISSUE, 30→ OBJECTIVE_ISSUE,

1→"""Implementation context staging directory utilities. 2→ 3→This module provides utilities for managing .erk/impl-context/ folder structures used 4→during plan submission workflows. The .erk/impl-context/ folder is committed to the 5→branch and contains the implementation plan, making it visible in the PR immediately. 6→ 7→Unlike .impl/ folders (ephemeral, local, never committed), .erk/impl-context/ folders are: 8→- Committed to the branch 9→- Visible in draft PR immediately 10→- Removed before implementation begins (Step 2d of plan-implement) 11→ 12→Folder structure: 13→.erk/impl-context/ 14→├── plan.md # Full plan content from GitHub issue 15→└── ref.json # Plan reference metadata (provider, plan_id, url, etc.) 16→""" 17→ 18→from __future__ import annotations 19→ 20→import json 21→import shutil 22→from pathlib import Path 23→ 24→from erk_shared.plan_store.planned_pr_lifecycle import IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR 25→ 26→ 27→def create_impl_context( 28→ plan_content: str, 29→ plan_id: str, 30→ url: str,
... [148 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Dispatch logic for one-shot execution via RemoteGitHub REST API. 2→ 3→Uses RemoteGitHub gateway to create branches, commit files, create PRs, 4→and dispatch workflows entirely via the GitHub REST API. 38→ """Validation completed (may have passed or failed checks). 43→ failed_count: Number of failed checks 44→ graph: Dependency graph of roadmap nodes (empty if parsing failed) 47→ validation_errors: Parser-level warnings from roadmap parsing 53→ failed_count: int 57→ validation_errors: list[str] 62→class ObjectiveValidationError: 65→ error: str 68→ObjectiveValidationResult = ObjectiveValidationSuccess | ObjectiveValidationError 127→ ObjectiveValidationError if unable to complete validation 134→ return ObjectiveValidationError(error=f"Issue #{issue_number} not found") 144→ phases, validation_errors = parse_roadmap(issue.body)

1→"""Helper functions for accessing context dependencies with LBYL checks. 2→ 3→This module provides getter functions that encapsulate the "Look Before You Leap" 4→pattern for accessing dependencies from the ErkContext. These functions: 5→ 6→1. Check that context is initialized 7→2. Return the typed dependency 8→3. Exit with clear error message if context is missing 9→ 10→This eliminates code duplication across kit CLI commands. 11→""" 12→ 13→from __future__ import annotations 14→ 15→from pathlib import Path 16→ 17→import click 18→ 19→from erk_shared.context.context import ErkContext 20→from erk_shared.context.types import LoadedConfig, NoRepoSentinel 21→from erk_shared.core.prompt_executor import PromptExecutor 22→from erk_shared.gateway.branch_manager.abc import BranchManager 23→from erk_shared.gateway.claude_installation.abc import ClaudeInstallation 24→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import Git 25→from erk_shared.gateway.github.abc import LocalGitHub 26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues 27→from erk_shared.gateway.time.abc import Time 28→from erk_shared.plan_store.backend import PlanBackend 29→ 30→

/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py

1→"""Context types for erk and erk-kits. 2→ 3→This module provides the core data types used by ErkContext: 4→- RepoContext: Repository discovery result 5→- NoRepoSentinel: Sentinel for when not in a repository 6→- GlobalConfig: Global erk configuration 7→- LoadedConfig: Repository-level configuration 8→- InteractiveAgentConfig: Configuration for interactive agent launches (Claude or Codex) 9→- AgentBackend: Type for agent backend selection 10→- PermissionMode: Generic permission mode for both Claude and Codex 11→""" 12→ 13→from __future__ import annotations 14→ 15→from dataclasses import dataclass 16→from functools import cache 17→from pathlib import Path 18→from typing import Literal, Self 19→ 20→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId 21→ 22→# Agent backend types 23→AgentBackend = Literal["claude", "codex"] 24→ 25→# Claude CLI permission modes: 26→# - "default": Default mode with permission prompts 27→# - "acceptEdits": Accept edits without prompts (--permission-mode acceptEdits) 28→# - "plan": Plan mode for exploration and planning (--permission-mode plan) 29→# - "bypassPermissions": Bypass all permissions (--permission-mode bypassPermissions) 30→ClaudePermissionMode = Literal["default", "acceptEdits", "plan", "bypassPermissions"]
... [225 lines omitted] ...
51→ raise ValueError(f"Unknown permission_mode: {permission_mode}") 75→ raise ValueError(f"Unknown permission_mode: {permission_mode}") 99→ raise ValueError(f"Unknown permission_mode: {permission_mode}")

session.tool_result:
1→"""Draft PR Lifecycle. 2→ 3→Draft PRs serve as the backing store for plans. Plans evolve through 4→lifecycle stages within a single PR. 5→ 6→Branch Files 7→------------ 8→Draft PR branches contain ``{IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR}/plan.md`` and 9→``{IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR}/ref.json``, committed before PR creation to avoid 10→GitHub's "empty branch" rejection. ``plan.md`` enables inline review 11→comments on the plan via the PR's "Files Changed" tab and gets replaced 12→when implementation begins. ``ref.json`` carries plan reference metadata 13→(provider, objective_id). 14→ 15→Stages 16→------ 17→ 18→0. One-Shot Dispatch (optional) 19→ One-shot creates a draft PR plan and dispatches to GitHub Actions 20→ for remote planning + implementation. The workflow updates the 21→ PR body through stages 1-3 below. 22→ 23→1. Plan Creation 24→ ``plan_save`` / ``PlannedPRBackend.create_plan()`` creates a draft PR. 25→ The body contains an optional AI-generated summary followed by the plan 26→ content collapsed in a <details> tag, then the plan-header metadata block 27→ and a checkout footer. The metadata block is self-delimiting via HTML 28→ comment markers, so no separator is needed. 29→ 30→ Body format::
... [146 lines omitted] ...
